title: Unusual flood of money from a veterans group roils Iowa Senate primary
description: VoteVets is spending millions to boost a Paralympic gold medalist over a state Senator who's seen as more liberal in a race that Democrats say they have a shot to win.

title: Takeaways from the nation’s oddest Senate primary
description: Nebraska Democrats knew they couldn’t unseat a GOP senator, so they backed an independent and fielded a candidate who promised to drop out if she won. Then it got complicated.

title: White House aims to link U.S. history and Christianity in 9-hour prayer festival
description: Pete Hegseth, Marco Rubio and Mike Johnson will speak at the unprecedented event, arguing that the founders wanted the U.S. to be explicitly Christian.

title: Trump arrives in China with lowered ambitions, hoping for deals
description: Beijing is set to press the president on Taiwan, and he has indicated an openness to rethinking the degree of U.S. support for the island, alarming its backers.

title: How the Trump administration has undermined the fight against public corruption
description: At least 15 former elected officials and co-conspirators with corruption offenses have been pardoned by President Trump in the last year, undermining the fight against public corruption.

title: Is the U.S. slipping into 'Competitive Authoritarianism?'
description: What form of government do we have in America now? Some scholars say it is no longer liberal democracy, but "competitive authoritarianism." NPR's Frank Langfitt explains the term and its origin.

title: Lawmakers question Pete Hegseth about rising cost of U.S. war against Iran
description: Lawmakers are pressing Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth about the growing cost of the war against Iran and the administration's plans to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Rep. Rosa DeLauro, D-Conn., on the cost of the war in Iran
description: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Democratic Rep. Rosa DeLauro of Connecticut about Pentagon spending on the U.S. war against Iran.

title: Powell pulls ahead in tight race for Nebraska's 2nd District
description: Denise Powell pulled ahead Tuesday night in a close race with John Cavanaugh in Tuesday's 2nd Congressional District primary in

title: Kash Patel lashes out as lawmakers question ‘excessive drinking’
description: The FBI director offered a brash defense to questions centering on a recent news report alleging that his “excessive drinking” impaired his ability to do the job.

title: Former private prison official to serve as acting ICE chief
description: Compared to the start of President Trump's second term, David Venturella takes over an agency with a larger workforce, more financial resources — and also a continued funding lapse.

title: Why the Iran war is hurting the economy
description: There isn’t much Congress or Trump can do about it, experts warn

title: Hegseth, Caine encounter intense bipartisan frustration with Iran war
description: As a ceasefire with Tehran falters, Republicans joined Democrats in pressing for details on the conflict’s costs and the administration’s plan to end it.

title: Omaha's U.S. House race tests candidates' ability to reach beyond party lines
description: Omaha, Neb., is home to a toss-up Congressional race this year. Voters have shown a willingness to vote for moderate candidates from both parties, but has the definition of a moderate now changed?

title: Trump shares post calling for Obama’s arrest
description: The repost on social media was among a flurry of late-night posts in which President Donald Trump railed against his political opponents.

title: The SPLC survived firebombs and death threats. Will it survive Trump 2.0?
description: The Southern Poverty Law Center is facing a serious threat from the Justice Department — and comes at a time when several former and current SPLC employees say the group is already deeply vulnerable.

title: Southern states rush to draw new Congressional districts after Supreme Court ruling
description: Across the South, some state legislatures are rushing to redraw Congressional district lines after the Supreme Court cleared the way. Black voters say their voices are at stake.

title: Alabama splits U.S. House primaries after court ruling; S.C. redistricting stalls
description: Alabama will hold a special primary election for four of its seven congressional districts, after the Supreme Court cleared the way — and as redistricting ripples through the South.

title: Justice Dept. charges shipping company in deadly Key Bridge collapse
description: Prosecutors accused the Singapore-based company and subsidiaries of fostering unsafe conditions that caused the Dali cargo ship to crash into the Baltimore bridge in 2024.

title: Trump’s approach on China has changed, now appears focused on getting along
description: In his first term, the president demanded fundamental shifts in China’s economic model. Now, he has copied parts of how Beijing does business.

title: A California billionaire is testing voters’ aversion to the ultrarich
description: Tom Steyer is running for California governor on an affordability agenda, testing voters’ willingness to set aside their aversion to billionaires.

title: Lawmaker pushes for more ICE oversight after indictment for visit
description: Rep. LaMonica McIver (D-New Jersey) plans to introduce a bill to strengthen scrutiny by members of Congress as her own legal battle is about to escalate.

title: The complicated history of American presidents visiting China
description: From Nixon visiting during the Cold War to Clinton navigating the optics of being welcomed in Tiananmen Square, presidential trips to China have always been fraught.

title: Virginia Democrats appeal to U.S. Supreme Court to save new House maps
description: Some top Virginia Democrats express little hope that the appeal will affect this November’s congressional elections and instead will focus on running in existing districts.

title: Supreme Court hands Alabama major boost in redistricting fight
description: Alabama legislators are hoping to boost the GOP in November’s midterm elections in the wake of last month’s Supreme Court decision weakening the Voting Rights Act.

title: House ethics panel pledges ‘full and thorough’ investigation of Rep. Cory Mills
description: The Republican, whom President Donald Trump has endorsed for another term, has denied domestic violence and other allegations and sought to portray them as politically motivated.

title: Alito extends temporary order allowing mail distribution of abortion pill
description: The justices are weighing whether to uphold an appeals court decision that means patients would need to pick up the pill in person.

title: Trump seeks to pause federal gas tax as prices soar amid Iran conflict
description: Congress would have to approve the proposal, and passing the savings on to drivers, rather than oil companies, would be difficult to enforce.

title: Nonprofit sues to stop Trump’s changes to Reflecting Pool, a historic site
description: President Donald Trump’s order to apply a blue-tinted surface to the Lincoln Memorial’s Reflecting Pool needed federal review, a D.C.-area foundation says.

title: In turf battle over AI, U.S. spy agencies vie for more sway than Commerce
description: As the White House grapples with cybersecurity threats from advanced artificial intelligence models, national security officials want more sway in AI regulation.

title: Trump says Iran ceasefire is on ‘life support,’ calls latest proposal ‘garbage’
description: Iran defended the proposal as “reasonable and generous,” as oil prices rose Monday morning in response to the continued stalemate.
